content: {"analyses": [{"source_url": "https://www.npjournal.org/article/S1555-4155%252823%252900395-1/fulltext", "source_title": "Intermittent Fasting: Exploring Approaches, Benefits, and ...", "source_type": "web", "source_quality": 0.7, "overall_relevance": 0.65, "year": 0, "authors": [], "venue": "", "doi": "", "atomic_facts": [{"statement": "Updated: April 7, 2026 Intermittent fasting is an eating plan that switches between fasting and eating on a regular schedule.", "direct_quote": "Updated: April 7, 2026 Intermittent fasting is an eating plan that switches between fasting and eating on a regular schedule.", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.65, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": []}, {"statement": "Johns Hopkins neuroscientist Mark Mattson, who has studied intermittent fasting for 25 years, explains this dietary approach.", "direct_quote": "Johns Hopkins neuroscientist Mark Mattson, who has studied intermittent fasting for 25 years, explains this dietary approach.", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.65, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": []}, {"statement": "Key Points - Intermittent fasting focuses on when you eat instead of how much or what you eat. - Research shows that intermittent fasting is...", "direct_quote": "Key Points - Intermittent fasting focuses on when you eat instead of how much or what you eat. - Research shows that intermittent fasting is a way to manage your weight and prevent \u2014 or even reverse \u2014 some forms of disease. - There are several fastin", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.65, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": []}], "evidence_summary": "Auto-extracted 3 facts."}]}
